![Matthew Turner](photo.png){.float-left height=210px}

Matthew is a quantitative social scientist interested in accelerating
translational research to develop sustainable real-world social systems. Matthew
got his opportunity to develop social science-powered public health
interventions in the context of the Dourados Indigenous Reserve as a Pandemic
Preparedness Hub fellow at Stanford Medicine with co-founder Iza Rezende.
Matthew has received several awards for his work, including the Cognitive
Science Society Conference’s Diversity and Social Inequality award for
demonstrating the practical importance of minority groups for accelerating
public health dissemination following an educational intervention, since
minority groups tend to be relatively more visible in their communities. Matthew
has developed and taught new courses for the Stanford Doerr School of
Sustainability, received his PhD in Cognitive and Information Sciences in 2021
from the University of California, Merced, holds a M.S. in Applied Physics from
Rice University and a dual B.S. in Mathematics and Physics from Syracuse
University. Professionally, Matthew developed cyberinfrastructure for high
performance model and data fusion for mountain hydrology and labor econometrics
as a research software developer and data engineer.
